DISCOVERY 612, HEARING HELD. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR DISCOVERY SANCTIONS PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 2023.030 IS GRANTED AS THE MOTION SEEKS AN ORDER BARRING PLAINTIFF'S TESTIMONY AT TRIAL. THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THE PREVIOUS ORDER WAS A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. SANCTIONS ARE IMPOSED AGAINST PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $880.30, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

LAW AND MOTION, 301, A R G U E D AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: PLAINTIFF MICHAEL ANDONIE'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD IS OFF CALENDAR. - MOTION SERVED JUNE 14, 2005 BY MAIL, SETTING HEARING DATE OF JUNE 23, 2005, WHICH IS NOT SUFFICIENT TIME UNDER CCP 1005)B) - THERE IS AN OST SETTING HEARING ON 6/23/05, BUT THE OST WAS NOT SIGNED UNTIL 6/17/05, MAKING SERVICE ON 6/14/05 IMPOSSIBLE. - IN ADDITION, THERE IS NO PROOF OF SERVICE OF ORDER SHORTENING TIME. THE PLAINTIFF OBJECTED TO THE SERVICE. JUDGE: JAMES L. WARREN, REPORTER: CHRISTINA PAXTON, CSR #1558

Off Calendar Nov-10-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Elizabeth MacDonald, Esq., specially appeared for Plaintiff
No other appearance.
Ruling:
Hearing held. Tentative Ruling adopted: Defendant's Motion For Discovery Sanctions Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 2023.030 is granted as the motion seeks an order barring Plaintiff's testimony at trial. The court finds that Plaintiff's failure to comply with the previous order was a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are imposed against Plaintiff in the amount of $880.30, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Off Calendar Jun-23-2005 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Douglas Fladseth, Esq., 707-545-2600 moving to withdraw as attorney of record for plaintiff Michael Andonie.
Michael Andonie, 916-223-5501, plaintiff and opposing party.
Court Call Appearance: Jamie Pearson. Esq. of Barry, Ubaldi, McPherson & Flesher, 916-635-2200 for defendant, Mercy San Juan Medical Center and Catholic Healthcare West.

RULING:
A R G U E D and the Court adopted its Tentative Ruling:
PLAINTIFF MICHAEL ANDONIE'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD IS OFF CALENDAR.
- MOTION SERVED JUNE 14, 2005 BY MAIL, SETTING HEARING DATE OF JUNE 23, 2005, WHICH IS NOT SUFFICIENT TIME UNDER CCP 1005)b)
- THERE IS AN OST SETTING HEARING ON 6/23/05, BUT THE OST WAS NOT SIGNED UNTIL 6/17/05,
MAKING SERVICE ON 6/14/05 IMPOSSIBLE.
- IN ADDITION, THERE IS NO PROOF OF SERVICE OF ORDER SHORTENING TIME.
THE PLAINTIFF OBJECTED TO THE SERVICE. Judge James L. Warren, Reporter Christina Paxton CSR# 1558. (301/JLW)
